name: Hanging Leg Raise
resistance_profile: Loads the lower-ab region via posterior pelvic tilt; curl the pelvis, not just the legs.
cues: Curl the pelvis up (not just hip-flex)
execution_steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, arms straight and body still.
Brace your abs and tilt your pelvis to stop any swinging.
Curl your pelvis up and raise your legs, leading with the pelvic tilt rather than just lifting the legs.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control back to a dead hang.
good_when: You want a challenging bodyweight ab exercise with a long range of motion.
You have the grip and shoulder strength to hang and control your bodyweight.
You've progressed beyond floor crunches and lying leg raises.
bad_when: You can't control the swing yet (regress to a hanging knee raise or lying leg raise).
Your grip fails before your abs do (use a captain's-chair or lying variation).
You only feel it in your hip flexors and haven't drilled the pelvic curl yet.
